revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蝦夷穴古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = '''Ezoana Kofun''' (蝦夷穴古墳) | image = [[File:Ezoana Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Mound and stone-chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|qid=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, Wada (Ezoana) | coordinates = {{Coord|37.282444444444444|N|140.39825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | region = Tōhoku | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|lt=Round kofun|de|Rundkofun|ja|円墳|zh|圓墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | diameter = 36 m | height = 4–5 m | built = Early 7th century CE | burial_facility = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|de|Korridorsteinkammer|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|zh|橫穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial_goods = Gilt-bronze ring-pommeled sword, bronze bowl, horse trappings, and numerous other artefacts | designation1 = Prefectural Historic Site (Fukushima) | designation1_date = 1 August 1958 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_caption = Location within Fukushima Prefecture }}
'''Ezoana Kofun''' (蝦夷穴古墳) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|lt=Round kofun|de|Rundkofun|ja|円墳|zh|圓墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|qid=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound has been designated a Prefectural Historic Site.
==Overview== The mound stands on a slight terrace on the west bank of the {{ill|Abukuma River|en|Abukuma River|qid=Q335853|lt=Abukuma River|1=Abukuma River}} in the southern part of the prefecture. Although the foot of the mound has been cut away, and the interior of the stone chamber was excavated during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the original scale can still be discerned. The mound is circular, measuring about 36 metres in diameter and 4–5 metres in height.<ref name="Board">Site information board, Sukagawa City Board of Education.</ref>
The burial chamber is a finely dressed {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|de|Korridorsteinkammer|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|zh|橫穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southeast. With a total length of roughly 11 metres, it is among the largest stone chambers known in the Tōhoku region.<ref name="Board"/> Grave goods recovered in Meiji-period excavations include a gilt-bronze ring-pommeled sword, a bronze bowl, and horse trappings.<ref>{{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}</ref>
On the basis of construction technique and assemblage, the mound is dated to the first half of the 7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref name="Board"/> Its large stone chamber and rich grave goods make it one of the representative mounded tombs of the region for this period.
The site was designated a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site in 1988.<ref name="Board"/>
==History of Investigation== * '''1885''' & '''1888''' – Stone chamber excavated; artefacts transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}.<ref>{{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}</ref> * '''1 August 1958''' – Designated a Prefectural Historic Site.<ref name="List">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/451668.pdf 福島県 国・県指定等文化財一覧]}}</ref>
==Burial Facility== [[File:Ezoana Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|center|Plan of the stone chamber]] [[File:Ezoana Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|center|Elevation of the stone chamber]]
The chamber consists of a main chamber, entrance, and passage (or antechamber). The upper part of the passage is missing. Principal dimensions are as follows:<ref>{{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}</ref> * Total length: approx. 11 m (reported as 8 m in some literature)<ref>{{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}</ref> * Main chamber: length 4.35 m, width 2.1 m (max.), height 2.8 m
The chamber is built of cut blocks of quartz-andesite welded tuff with exceptionally large blocks used for the back wall and ceiling.<ref name="Board"/>
Grave goods recovered include the gilt-bronze ring-pommeled sword, a bronze bowl, beads (''magatama'' and tubular beads), a knife, gilt-bronze harness fittings, glass beads, and horse teeth, all now held by the Tokyo National Museum.<ref>{{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}</ref>
<gallery> Ezoana Kofun, genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Ezoana Kofun, genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Ezoana Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward entrance) Ezoana Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance (toward main chamber) Ezoana Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward main chamber) Ezoana Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|Mound </gallery>
==Historic Designation== ===Fukushima Prefecture Cultural Property=== * '''Historic Site''' – ''Ezoana Kofun'' (designated 1 August 1958).<ref name="List"/>
==Related Facilities== * {{ill|Sukagawa City Museum|de|Stadtmuseum Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市立博物館|zh|須賀川市立博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa City Museum|1=Sukagawa City Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664795}} * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} – Repository of the excavated artefacts.
==References== {{reflist}}
==Bibliography== * Sukagawa City Board of Education (1989). ''Site information board''. * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1993|title=福島県の地名|chapter=蝦夷穴古墳|series={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|de|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|zh|日本歷史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Name Series|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 7|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=川崎純徳|year=1989|chapter=蝦夷穴古墳|title={{ill|Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|de|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|zh|日本古墳大辭典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654087}}|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=福島雅儀|year=2002|chapter=蝦夷穴古墳|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|de|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|zh|續 日本古墳大辭典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|qid=Q135524461}}|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Ezoana Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
==See also== * {{ill|Inakodate Kofun|de|Inakodate Kofun|ja|稲古舘古墳|zh|稻古館古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inakodate Kofun|1=Inafurudate Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q111142365}} {{Kofun navbar}}
==External links== {{commonscat|Ezoana Kofun}} * [https://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/bunka_sports/bunka_geijyutsu/bunkazai/1002517.html Ezoana Kofun – Sukagawa City official site]
{{translated page|ja|蝦夷穴古墳|version=101082759|comment=Imported full ja history}}
